title: ERROR: Assert `existing_cell' failed in passes/techmap/abc9.cc:516
body:
## Steps to reproduce the issue

Using testcase2.il from [abc9.tar.gz](https://github.com/YosysHQ/yosys/files/4055235/abc9.tar.gz), run `yosys -p "abc9 -lut +/ecp5/abc9_5g.lut" testcase2.il`.

## Expected behavior

ABC9 completes correctly.

## Actual behavior

```
2. Executing ABC9 pass (technology mapping using ABC9).

2.1. Extracting gate netlist of module `\top' to `<abc-temp-dir>/input.xaig'..

2.1.1. Executing SCC pass (detecting logic loops).
Found 0 SCCs in module top.
Found 0 SCCs.

2.1.2. Executing AIGMAP pass (map logic to AIG).
Module top: replaced 114 cells with 795 new cells, skipped 3 cells.
  replaced 2 cell types:
       1 $_OR_
     113 $_MUX_
  not replaced 3 cell types:
       1 $_AND_
       1 $_DFF_P_
       1 $__DFFS_PP0_

2.1.3. Executing XAIGER backend.
<suppressed ~5 debug messages>
Extracted 341 AND gates and 5018 wires to a netlist network with 4112 inputs and 288 outputs.

2.1.4. Executing ABC9.
Running ABC command: <yosys-exe-dir>/yosys-abc -s -f <abc-temp-dir>/abc.script 2>&1
ABC: ABC command line: "source <abc-temp-dir>/abc.script".
ABC:
ABC: + read_lut <yosys-exe-dir>/../share/yosys/ecp5/abc9_5g.lut
ABC: + read_box <yosys-exe-dir>/../share/yosys/dummy.box
ABC: + &read <abc-temp-dir>/input.xaig
ABC: + &ps
ABC: <abc-temp-dir>/input : i/o =   4112/    288  and =     341  lev =   10 (0.82)  mem = 0.07 MB  box = 0  bb = 0      ABC: + &scorr
ABC: Warning: The network is combinational.
ABC: + &sweep
ABC: + &dc2
ABC: + &dch -f
ABC: + &ps
ABC: <abc-temp-dir>/input : i/o =   4112/    288  and =     668  lev =    7 (0.81)  mem = 0.08 MB  ch =  109  box = 0  bb = 0
ABC: + &if -v
ABC: K = 7. Memory (bytes): Truth =    0. Cut =   60. Obj =  140. Set =  636. CutMin = no
ABC: Node =     668.  Ch =   109.  Total mem =    0.75 MB. Peak cut mem =    0.00 MB.
ABC: P:  Del =  671.00.  Ar =     121.0.  Edge =      347.  Cut =     1568.  T =     0.00 sec
ABC: P:  Del =  671.00.  Ar =     121.0.  Edge =      347.  Cut =     1568.  T =     0.00 sec
ABC: P:  Del =  671.00.  Ar =     121.0.  Edge =      350.  Cut =     1580.  T =     0.00 sec
ABC: F:  Del =  671.00.  Ar =     121.0.  Edge =      353.  Cut =     1541.  T =     0.00 sec
ABC: A:  Del =  671.00.  Ar =     121.0.  Edge =      347.  Cut =     1511.  T =     0.00 sec
ABC: A:  Del =  671.00.  Ar =     121.0.  Edge =      347.  Cut =     1565.  T =     0.00 sec
ABC: Total time =     0.00 sec
ABC: + &mfs
ABC: Timing manager is given but there is no GIA of boxes.
ABC: Error: Abc_FrameUpdateGia(): Tranformation has failed.
ABC: + &ps -l
ABC: <abc-temp-dir>/input : i/o =   4112/    288  and =     348  lev =    7 (0.81)  mem = 0.07 MB  box = 0  bb = 0      ABC: Mapping (K=6)  :  lut =    112  edge =     347  lev =    2 (0.39)  mem = 0.02 MB
ABC: LUT = 112 : 2=1 0.9 %  3=106 94.6 %  4=0 0.0 %  5=3 2.7 %  6=2 1.8 %  Ave = 3.10
ABC: + &write -n <abc-temp-dir>/output.aig
ABC: + time
ABC: elapse: 0.03 seconds, total: 0.03 seconds
<suppressed ~8808 debug messages>
Removed 584 unused cells and 8423 unused wires.

2.1.5. Re-integrating ABC9 results.
ERROR: Assert `existing_cell' failed in passes/techmap/abc9.cc:516.
```

My suspicion is it's the `$__DFFS_PP0` that's tripping up ABC9, however, when you run `yosys -p "abc9" testcase2.il` you get a different error:

```
2. Executing ABC9 pass (technology mapping using ABC9).

2.1. Extracting gate netlist of module `\top' to `<abc-temp-dir>/input.xaig'..
ERROR: Abort in passes/techmap/abc9.cc:265.
```

cc @eddiehung
